top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 146, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
90ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Peaks Challenge Falls Creek, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
13,123ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Peaks Challenge Falls Creek's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Peaks Challenge Falls Creek. Train it until it feels boring.
13,123ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 146 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Peaks Challenge Falls Creek's paved alpine roads through Victorian High Country forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 146 miles.
146 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 13,123ft of climbing on paved alpine roads through Victorian High Country does to your power file on race day.
Low-cadence force work for 13,123ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Peaks Challenge Falls Creek’s 146 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Peaks Challenge Falls Creek. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 146 miles of paved alpine roads through victorian high country in Falls Creek, Victoria, Australia.
